factors.name: Tech selloff momentum
factors.summary: NQ declined 4.60% for the week, with 5 consecutive down sessions. Nasdaq led Friday's decline at -0.24% while Dow and S&P 500 were flat, signaling tech-specific weakness.

factors.name: AI capex concerns escalating
factors.summary: Oracle posted worst week since 2001 dot-com bust amid 'AI financing concerns'—surging capex, negative free cash flow, and $130B debt pile. ServiceNow/Salesforce rallied on OpenAI threat weakening, but Oracle's weakness signals hyperscaler ROI doubts spreading.

factors.name: Semiconductor sector rout
factors.summary: Micron sank 6% on Friday; ON Semiconductor hit worst day since 2020. Chip stocks globally under pressure, core NQ constituent weakness.

factors.name: Bond yields falling, real rates easing
factors.summary: Treasury yields falling as inflation worries ease (PCE data 'broadly in-line' per news). Lower nominal yields typically bullish for long-duration growth, partially offsetting equity risk-off.

factors.name: Fed pivot narrative gaining traction
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's hawkish tone helping yields lower (bond market repricing inflation); Kashkari now projects one rate hike vs prior expectations. Mixed signals on policy direction.

factors.name: Geopolitical tail risk: US-Iran escalation
factors.summary: US struck Iran after ceasefire violation in Strait of Hormuz; trilateral Israel-US-Lebanon framework signed but Hezbollah resistance high. War risk premium back in focus; energy volatility could spill into equities.

factors.name: Valuation stress amid capex doubt
factors.summary: Wall Street reckoning with '$270B AI speculation machine' reversal—question: 'what are we getting for all this AI spending?' Long-duration growth multiples under pressure.

factors.name: AI model competition tightening
factors.summary: China's Zhipu GLM 5.2 'closing in' on US models; OpenAI/Anthropic facing export controls. ROI per dollar tilting away from pure capex plays to efficiency innovators like Zhipu and open-source.

factors.name: Dollar strength moderating
factors.summary: USD mixed on the week; CAD trimming losses. Weakening USD typically supportive of NQ international revenue exposure but not a primary driver this cycle.

factors.name: Healthcare rotation out of tech
factors.summary: AbbVie, Eli Lilly, J&J at all-time highs on Friday; investors fleeing volatility in tech. Sector rotation away from NQ heavyweights gaining momentum.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in a bear-phase correction as AI capex euphoria collapses amid mounting ROI concerns. Oracle's worst week since 2001 dot-com bust crystallizes doubts: mega-cap hyperscalers are spending aggressively (OpenAI, chip orders, cloud buildouts) but concrete revenue translation is opaque. Semiconductor weakness (Micron, ON Semi) and sector rotation into defensive healthcare signal risk-off sentiment entrenched. While Treasury yields are falling and the Fed may avoid aggressive hikes, this is a growth multiple compression story—lower rates help *eventually*, but near-term earnings revisions and capex guidance downgrades are the catalyst. NQ has now fallen 4.60% for the week; the move is in mid-stage (PHASE 2), not yet capitulation or reversal.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (jobs report) next week—strong print could re-ignite hawkish repricing
ISM Manufacturing (next week) on AI semiconductor demand
Eurozone inflation releases—external growth weakness could hurt NQ earnings
Oracle, Micron, NVIDIA earnings and capex guidance calls (likely next 2 weeks)
OpenAI confidential IPO filing—any timeline update could shift risk sentiment
Fed speakers (Warsh, Powell) on policy path and inflation trajectory
screening.risk_warning: If PCE/CPI data prints cold (deflation surprise), real yields plummet sharply, and long-duration tech multiple expansion accelerates, NQ could reverse hard. Additionally, if a major hyperscaler (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) announces blockbuster capex ROI metrics or new AI revenue lines, capitulation selling could reverse into strong short-covering relief rally. Geopolitical de-escalation (Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, Iran talks progress) could also trigger risk-on reversal.
screening.entry_guidance: NQ is in mid-phase decline; avoid chasing shorts. Better to wait for retest of 29200–29300 resistance (where initial sellers took profits) before re-entering short, or wait for capitulation lower (sub-28500) and multi-day reversal candle before considering longs. High-impact NFP and earnings catalysts this week make position-building risky near-term.

factors.name: Tech earnings disappointment & AI capex skepticism
factors.summary: Oracle worst week since 2001 dot-com bust; 'What exactly are we getting for all this AI spending?' narrative emerging. Hyperscaler Q1 results scrutinized for ROI on massive capex outlays; no clear earnings acceleration visible.

factors.name: Nasdaq momentum deterioration
factors.summary: NAS100 down 4.60% for the week, declined in each of past 5 trading sessions; worst week for tech stocks in ~1 year. Current 4h: -0.75%, 24h: -0.58% — consistent downward pressure, not bounce.

factors.name: Real yields & Fed rate uncertainty
factors.summary: Kashkari now projects one rate hike in 2026 (vs. prior expectations of cuts); US-Iran tensions and AI capex concerns cited as triggers. Higher real yields compress long-duration growth multiples — direct headwind for NDX.

factors.name: Treasury yield stabilization & USD strength
factors.summary: Bond yields falling on inflation pop narrative, but Warsh's 'tough talk' on inflation supporting yields at elevated levels. Strong dollar pressures international revenues of tech exporters (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL have 50%+ FX exposure).

factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran strike confirmed)
factors.summary: US confirmed retaliatory strike on Iran after ship attack in Strait of Hormuz; ceasefire framework with Lebanon still fragile. Risk-off backdrop pressures risk assets; oil volatility (settled $69.23, -3.74% day) raises energy/supply uncertainty.

factors.name: Semiconductor sector weakness
factors.summary: Micron worst day since 2020; ON Semiconductor sharply down (Synaptics M&A skepticism); NVDA not mentioned in positive light. Chip stocks lagging despite AI narrative; demand visibility deteriorating.

factors.name: Positive: Healthcare rotation & defensive positioning
factors.summary: AbbVie, Eli Lilly, J&J hitting all-time highs as investors rotate from tech. Signals risk-off mood but also some institutional repositioning — short-term headwind for growth/tech.

factors.name: SpaceX Nasdaq listing & passive inflows
factors.summary: SpaceX set to join NAS100 under new fast-track rule; expected to generate passive buying. But $25bn bond deal and warning signals in SpaceX bonds suggest caution; likely minor +1-2% pop, not broad NDX catalyst.

factors.name: Meta, AI model competition & regulatory clouds
factors.summary: Meta exploring Polymarket/Kalshi partnerships; Anthropic Mythos AI released to 'trusted' US orgs (export controls). CFTC probing Polymarket for deceptive marketing. Mixed sentiment; no clear earnings driver yet.

factors.name: Prior bias mismatch
factors.summary: 2026-06-27 00:06 read: SELL 6/10 (no confirmation triggered) — today's move (-0.75% 4h, -0.58% 24h, weak week) IS the confirmation. First-cycle sell setup now entering Phase 2 with price action validating thesis.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq has entered a confirmed downtrend after week-long tech sell-off driven by AI capex ROI skepticism (Oracle collapse) and rising real yields (Kashkari hawkish, inflation not cooling enough). Geopolitical risk (US-Iran strike) and semiconductor weakness (Micron, ON Semi) reinforce defensive rotation into healthcare. SpaceX listing provides modest offset but insufficient to reverse the broader sell-off narrative. Prior SELL 6/10 bias is now validated by price action and deteriorating momentum.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (Non-Farm Payroll) — early next week, key Fed rate path indicator
ISM Manufacturing PMI — market sensitivity to growth/capex outlook
CPI / Core CPI release — critical for real yields and Fed credibility on inflation control
FOMC minutes or Fed speaker commentary — Warsh, Powell positioning on rate path
NVDA earnings / guidance (if within 72h) — semiconductor demand bellwether
Ongoing US-Iran escalation or de-escalation clarity — risk-on/off regime shift
Meta, AAPL, GOOGL earnings/guidance updates — mega-cap earnings delivery on AI/capex promises
screening.risk_warning: If geopolitical tensions de-escalate (US-Iran ceasefire holds), real yields fall unexpectedly on softer CPI, or NVDA/mega-cap earnings beat with bullish AI capex commentary, NAS100 could reverse sharply higher (+2-3% in 1-2 sessions). Conversely, if move extends >2% lower on next leg, late sellers could trigger cascading stops.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (5-day down trend, 4.60% weekly loss already posted). Wait for pullback retest of 29250–29300 resistance before shorting; or enter on fresh breakdown below 28950 support with tight stops at 29100. Avoid chasing lower until key economic data (NFP/CPI) clarifies Fed and real yield trajectory.

factors.name: Tech sector momentum collapse
factors.summary: Nasdaq down 4.60% for the week with declines in each of the past 5 trading sessions. MarketWatch reports 'one of the worst weeks for tech stocks of a year' as AI spending ROI questions escalate and Oracle recorded worst week since 2001 dot-com bust.

factors.name: AI capex concerns intensifying
factors.summary: Wall Street confronting 'what exactly are we getting for all this AI spending' (MarketWatch). Oracle's negative free cash flow, $130B debt pile, and surging spend driving stock weakness. Kashkari now projects only one rate hike vs. previous dovish stance, citing AI buildup doubts.

factors.name: Real yields and duration sensitivity
factors.summary: 10Y Treasury yields falling as inflation expectations ease (core PCE data mentioned easing Fed hawkish bets). Lower nominal rates typically supportive for long-duration tech, but real yield trajectory unclear; conflicting signals from geopolitical risk.

factors.name: Geopolitical tail risk: Iran-US escalation
factors.summary: U.S. confirmed retaliatory strike on Iran after ceasefire violation claims. Rubio outlines Lebanon framework but 'major obstacles remain.' Oil recovered briefly on attack news but crude still down 8.5% weekly, signaling market downplaying immediate risk-off.

factors.name: Sector rotation out of tech
factors.summary: Healthcare stocks (AbbVie, Eli Lilly, J&J) surging to all-time highs; investors fleeing tech into defensive haven plays. Signals loss of confidence in mega-cap growth narrative.

factors.name: SpaceX Nasdaq entry + passive flows
factors.summary: SpaceX joining Nasdaq-100 on July 7 expected to trigger wave of passive fund inflows (multiple sources). Single-name catalyst and index reweighting could provide near-term bid.

factors.name: AI commodity/chip demand resilient
factors.summary: Electronics exports booming across Vietnam, South Korea, Thailand (all 1Q26 data cited); AI-driven semiconductor demand offsetting macro slowdown. DBS forecasts South Korea 50-60% YoY export growth on AI chip strength.

factors.name: China macro softening
factors.summary: China May industrial profits slowing despite export support (Bloomberg). Weak domestic demand backdrop could pressure Nasdaq components with heavy China revenue exposure (AAPL, MSFT).

factors.name: VIX/volatility regime
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in news window, but sell-off intensity and cross-sector rotation suggest elevated but not panic levels. Market functioning, not frozen.

factors.name: Dollar direction
factors.summary: Greenback ended mixed for week; USD/JPY near 162 multi-decade high on BoJ hike, but core USD momentum softening on PCE data. No clear directional bias for NDX.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq has rolled over hard this week (-4.60%) as the market shifts from AI euphoria to ROI skepticism. Mega-cap tech earnings cycle looms but forward guidance on cloud capex and AI spending efficiency is now the critical test—Oracle's flop exemplifies investor unease over mounting capex with unclear returns. Healthcare and other defensive sectors are outperforming, signaling rotation away from duration risk. Geopolitical escalation (Iran strike, Lebanon tensions) adds tail risk, though oil markets are pricing it as manageable. The silver lining is semiconductor demand remains robust on AI chip orders, and SpaceX's fast-track Nasdaq entry (July 7) could provide liquidity tailwind to tech flow.
catalysts_ahead: NFP (Non-Farm Payroll) next Friday – will reset Fed rate-hike odds
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL earnings guidance – critical capex/demand commentary
PCE inflation data (if fresh release) – impacts real yield repricing
SpaceX Nasdaq inclusion (July 7) – potential passive inflow catalyst
FOMC member commentary (Fed Chair Warsh talking hawkish inflation support) – sets tone for rate path
ISM Manufacturing PMI – corporate health and capital spending intentions
screening.risk_warning: Move already extended 4.60% weekly with 5 down days in a row (PHASE 3). Late-stage sell-off vulnerable to capitulation bounce or bargain-hunting snap-back if earnings guidance surprises to upside or Fed signals pivot. Iran-Israel escalation (material spike in oil/VIX) could flip bias sharply bullish on safe-haven dollar/bonds repricing. Geopolitical de-escalation (Rubio framework progress) also bullish for risk-on reversal.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended and late-stage; avoid chasing shorts. Wait for retest of 29300+ resistance or confirmed reversal pattern before fresh entry. If pullback occurs to 28900-29000, that offers better risk/reward for bears with damaged technicals as confirmation.

factors.name: Tech selloff momentum
factors.summary: NDX down 4.60% for the week with declines in 5 consecutive sessions; Nasdaq led Friday's decline at -0.24%. Move is already underway but not yet exhausted.

factors.name: AI capex spending doubts
factors.summary: Oracle had worst week since 2001 dot-com bust as AI financing concerns escalate; questions about ROI on massive datacenter investments now front-and-center per MarketWatch. ServiceNow/Salesforce rallied as OpenAI threat weakens, signaling sector rotation away from pure AI infrastructure plays.

factors.name: Fed tightening shift
factors.summary: Minneapolis Fed's Kashkari now projects one rate hike this year (up from prior guidance), citing U.S.-Iran tensions and AI buildup uncertainty. Bond yields falling but real yields remain sticky; long-duration growth tech extremely sensitive to any rate hike signal.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk escalation
factors.summary: U.S. strikes Iran in retaliation; ceasefire violations in Strait of Hormuz reigniting war-risk premium conversations. Markets are absorbing this as tail-risk despite oil settling at $69.23 (down 8.5% weekly); uncertainty typically caps multiple expansion.

factors.name: Treasury yields and real rates
factors.summary: 10-year yields falling and USD softening after PCE surprise; however, real yields remain elevated enough to keep growth multiple compression pressure. Falling nominal yields is modest tailwind but insufficient vs capex uncertainty headwinds.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings cycle timing
factors.summary: No major earnings catalysts in next 24-72h window; next week brings NFP and ISM Manufacturing data. Absence of positive earnings surprises removes near-term bid for mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Sector rotation into Healthcare
factors.summary: AbbVie, Eli Lilly, Johnson & Johnson hitting all-time highs Friday as investors rotate OUT of tech; classic risk-off pattern. Suggests institutional conviction in tech pullback is real.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO hype and float risk
factors.summary: SpaceX joining NDX on July 7; massive passive inflows expected but also noted as 'terrible buy' in short-term per MarketWatch. New inclusion noise is near-term distraction, not directional signal.

factors.name: Crypto weakness (BTC $60K test)
factors.summary: Bitcoin holding above $60K but old ETH wallets showing negative whale profitability (first time since 2019); risk-off sentiment in crypto aligns with tech weakness. NQ-BTC correlation intact.

factors.name: China AI closing gap on U.S.
factors.summary: Zhipu GLM 5.2 closing in on OpenAI/Anthropic on efficiency metrics; China industrial profits resilient. Longer-term threat to U.S. AI moat and capex justification narratives.

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq 100 is in the second week of a tech-sector reassessment driven by AI capex ROI doubts (Oracle collapse, hyperscaler guidance questions) and Fed pivot hawkishness (Kashkari rate hike signal). Geopolitical tail-risk (U.S.-Iran strikes) is adding noise but not yet dominating; oil's 42% YTD decline shows markets are pricing reduced war-premium impact. Sector rotation into defensive healthcare is a clear tell of institutional de-risking from mega-cap growth. The 4.60% weekly decline and 5-session down streak indicate PHASE 2 momentum, but Friday's -0.75% 4h move is modest—pullback risk is real before next leg down.
catalysts_ahead: NFP (U.S. non-farm payroll) — next Friday, early July, impacts Fed rate path
ISM Manufacturing — next week, real-time capex demand signal
FOMC minutes or Fed speaker commentary — Fed tightening/pausing signals
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL earnings guidance updates (dates TBD in early July)
PCE/Core PCE inflation data — key Fed repricing trigger
Oil volatility on fresh Iran/Middle East headlines — correlation drag on NDX
screening.risk_warning: If PCE/Core PCE unexpectedly cool in next release (dovish repricing), or if mega-cap earnings beat expectations with strong forward guidance on cloud/AI capex ROI, the SELL bias flips to NEUTRAL/BUY. A failed breakdown below 28,800 support with a V-bounce could also signal exhaustion and pullback completion—watch for 2-3 green days and retest of 29,300–29,500 as re-entry zone. Geopolitical de-escalation (U.S.-Iran ceasefire breakthrough) would remove tail-risk and allow multiple re-expansion.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (underway, not yet exhausted). Avoid chasing shorts into Friday's weakness. Better entry on a retest of 29,300–29,500 resistance (old Friday highs) if momentum turns negative, or on a failed bounce at 28,900 support. Wait 1-2 more days for stabilization signal before committing size. Confidence only justified if PCE/Fed speakers re-affirm tightening bias over weekend/early week.

factors.name: Tech rotation underway
factors.summary: Equal-weighted S&P 500 outperformed cap-weighted by widest margin in 6 years this week; mega-cap tech facing structural rotation as AI capex ROI questioned. Oracle had worst week since 2001 dot-com bust on negative FCF and $130B debt concerns.

factors.name: AI capex efficiency concerns
factors.summary: Wall Street confronting 'what are we getting for all this AI spending' after one of worst weeks for tech in a year. SpaceX bond deal flagging investor caution on AI frenzy; Lenovo CFO comments suggest capex scrutiny intensifying.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings expectations softening
factors.summary: No major earnings from AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, TSLA in news window; forward guidance on cloud capex (AWS, Azure, GCP) absent. Absence of bullish guidance into rotation is structural negative.

factors.name: Continued downside momentum in NQ
factors.summary: NASDAQ down 0.24% Friday, down 4.60% for week, declined 5 consecutive sessions. Persistent weakness despite modest daily moves suggests capitulation phase forming rather than capitulation complete.

factors.name: Real yields / Fed policy neutrality
factors.summary: No FOMC surprise or inflation data in last 24h; gold rebounded on 'falling US yields' per FXStreet, but nominal yield data sparse. Fed expected to hold; no clear hawkish or dovish catalyst present.

factors.name: Macro shocks (Iran, geopolitics, oil)
factors.summary: US struck Iran; oil fell to $69.23 (down 8.5% week, 42.6% from peak). Geopolitical volatility elevated but oil weakness is deflationary for tech; no immediate rate hike risk. Removes tail-risk hedge bid.

factors.name: Dollar direction
factors.summary: Greenback ends mixed for week per FXStreet; USD/JPY near 162, USD/CNH stalled at 6.8020. No strong dollar rally pressuring NQ-heavy international revenue; but no meaningful weakness either.

factors.name: Crypto correlation (risk-off proxy)
factors.summary: Bitcoin near $60k (not provided exact, but CoinDesk mentions 'steadies near $60k'); Solana weakness noted. Crypto underperformance typically signals risk-off in equities; correlation intact.

factors.name: VIX / volatility structure
factors.summary: No VIX or VXN data in news window; however, extended tech selloff with modest daily moves suggests vol structure stable but sentiment deteriorating gradually rather than spiking.

factors.name: Bias history consistency
factors.summary: Three prior reads: SELL5/10 at 04:53, 07:49, 10:07 UTC on 2026-06-27, each with 'no confirmation triggered.' Today's -0.75% 4h / -0.58% 24h aligns with bias but move remains shallow.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in the midst of a structural rotation away from mega-cap tech into small/mid-cap value. Oracle's worst week since 2001 and Wall Street's growing skepticism over AI capex ROI have triggered a 4.6% weekly decline and 5-session losing streak. While daily moves remain modest (-0.75% 4h), the persistent weakness and lack of bullish earnings guidance signal this is Phase 3 of a bearish retracement, not a capitulation low. Geopolitical shocks (Iran, oil down 42.6% from peak) have actually relieved recession fears and deflationary pressure, removing a potential tech multiple support.
catalysts_ahead: NFP (Non-Farm Payroll) — likely next week; labor market data expected to confirm 'soft landing' or signal Fed hold
ISM Manufacturing — early next week; sentiment on manufacturing demand vs. AI-driven services
SpaceX Nasdaq 100 inclusion (July 7) — potential mega-flows but no immediate catalyst this week
FOMC Minutes or Fed speakers — any hawkish tone re-prices real yields upward, tech headwind
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, TSLA) — no dates published in news, but Q2 season likely weeks away; current silence is negative for near-term bid
screening.risk_warning: If mega-cap tech announces surprise capex or earnings beat with strong forward guidance (e.g., Microsoft Azure acceleration, NVDA demand surge), or if Fed signals imminent rate cut, rotation could reverse quickly. Alternatively, if broad-market macroeconomic data (jobs report, CPI) disappoint and spark 'risk-off' flight to Nasdaq mega-caps (QQQ, MSFT, AAPL), this bearish bias would be invalidated. Current move is extended 4-5 days; any sharp intraday reversal or consolidation above 29,400 would require reassessment.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 3 (extended after 5-session decline); avoid chasing shorts. Wait for retest of 29,400 resistance or a daily close back above 29,200 before considering fresh long entry. For shorts: entry on intraday rallies to 29,300–29,400 with stop at 29,500 is preferable to current levels. Key: confirmation from either mega-cap earnings weakness or Fed hawkish pivot needed to justify further downside conviction.

factors.name: Real yields and duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Long-duration tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL) extremely sensitive to real rate moves. With inflation upside risks and term premium potentially re-pricing higher, growth multiple compression risk is material.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in early Phase 2 of a downtrend after NASDAQ fell 4.60% this week (five consecutive daily declines), driven by tech earnings disappointments (Oracle worst week since 2001), AI capex sustainability doubts, and mega-cap valuation stress at 'most expensive market in US history' levels. Rotation from mega-cap tech to equal-weighted/cyclical names is accelerating, with semiconductor cost pressures and memory price inflation squeezing margins. However, FedEx demand proxy remains solid, and SpaceX Nasdaq inclusion on July 7 offers near-term passive fund inflows; ECB inflation warnings and geopolitical residual volatility (Iran ceasefire fragile) keep real yields elevated and growth multiples vulnerable. Bias has remained consistently SELL across three recent reads but lacks hard confirmations—suggesting analysts see downside but lack clear breakdown conviction.
catalysts_ahead: SpaceX Nasdaq 100 inclusion (July 7) — $B in passive inflows, tech sector tailwind
US inflation data (CPI, PCE release timing not specified but critical in current macro backdrop)
FOMC meeting / Fed communications (no date given; assume mid-late July)
Mega-cap earnings season continuation (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN guidance critical)
Geopolitical escalation risk (Iran-US, Hezbollah unresolved, Strait of Hormuz volatility)
Real yield/10-year Treasury moves (inverse to NDX duration sensitivity)
screening.risk_warning: Move is already -0.75% in 4h / -0.58% in 24h; if NAS100 closes above 29400 with positive close or FedEx demand signals strengthen broader economic resilience narrative, short thesis breaks. Passive fund inflows from SpaceX inclusion (July 7) could reverse sentiment if tech sentiment stabilizes. Fed dovish surprise or surprise inflation cool would flip bias to BUY. Real yield spike (>2.5% on 10-year TIPS) would accelerate downside; unexpected real yield fall could pivot to rally.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already in progress (Phase 2, -0.75% today). Wait for retest of 29400 resistance or 29200-29300 zone consolidation before considering fresh shorts; if closes decisively below 28850, consider fresh SELL into bounces toward 29100-29200. Long entry only if breaks above 29400 with volume and closes there; too early to chase shorts in extended move without confirmation at resistance.

factors.name: AI Trade Reassessment
factors.summary: Technology stocks closed volatile week sharply lower as investors reassess sustainability of AI trade per Bloomberg Intelligence. Concerns over rising capex, memory pricing, and return-on-investment starting to show in price action.

factors.name: Price Action: Intraday Weakness
factors.summary: 4h change -0.75%, 24h change -0.58%. Nasdaq down 4.60% for the week with five consecutive daily declines. Move is established but not explosive, consistent with Phase 2 reassessment.

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq-100 is caught in a Phase 2 reassessment of the AI trade. While mega-cap tech earnings and capex remain central to the NDX story, mounting evidence of rising memory costs, valuation extremes (Grantham's 'most expensive market' call), and a structural rotation out of mega-caps into equal-weight are shifting sentiment. Geopolitical risks (Iran, Strait of Hormuz) add macro uncertainty. However, FedEx guidance and AI-driven GDP growth keep long-term bulls anchored. The move down is established (4.6% weekly loss, 5 down days) but not yet extended; expect chop and possible pullback test.
catalysts_ahead: Upcoming FOMC meeting / rate decision (exact date not in news, but typically scheduled)
Major NDX component earnings: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA guidance on cloud capex and AI demand
PCE or CPI release (inflation data critical for real yield repricing)
USMCA trade renegotiations update (affects tech supply chains, tariffs on semiconductors/autos)
SpaceX Nasdaq-100 inclusion effective July 7 (passive inflow mechanics, but single catalyst is limited)
Potential antitrust/regulatory news from DOJ, FTC, or EU DMA against AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL
screening.risk_warning: Move could be invalidated if: (1) next mega-cap earnings beat expectations and guide sharply higher on AI capex ROI; (2) Fed signals imminent rate cuts, driving long-duration multiple re-rating; (3) U.S.–Iran ceasefire holds and crude stabilizes, removing geopolitical risk premium; (4) PCE surprise comes in cool, easing inflation fears and supporting growth narrative. Current SELL bias is tactical/intermediate; upside breakout above 29500 on strong volume would signal Phase 1 reversal to BUY.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is established but not extended; avoid chasing shorts here. Consider entry on retest/bounce to 29300–29400 resistance band, or wait for clear break below 28900 support with volume. Confidence is held at 5 due to conflicting signals (valuation bearish, macro growth bullish) and lack of follow-through on prior SELL reads.

factors.name: Tech Multiple Compression
factors.summary: Jeremy Grantham warns 'This is the most expensive market in American history' citing AI valuations; Bloomberg reports 'Tech Slump Deepens' with concerns over rising semiconductor costs, memory pricing, and unsustainable capex. Oracle worst week since 2001 dot-com bust signals investor reassessment of AI spending ROI.

factors.name: AI Capex Sustainability Concerns
factors.summary: Multiple sources flag AI-driven capex as potentially overextended: Oracle's negative free cash flow, $130B debt pile, and surging spend; Bloomberg reports hyperscalers burning cash on infrastructure that may not generate returns fast enough. Memory shortage adding cost pressures for smaller players.

factors.name: Semiconductor Cost Inflation
factors.summary: Memory pricing surge is squeezing both mega-caps (who raise prices) and SMBs (who cannot); 'Memory shortage shaking Apple and Microsoft is existential crisis for smaller players.' Micron beat earnings but finished week red, suggesting guidance warned on margin pressure ahead.

factors.name: Macro Inflation Risks Resurging
factors.summary: ECB's Schnabel warns 'Upside Inflation Risks Despite Peace Deal' despite US-Iran ceasefire hopes; Europe hit by brutal heat wave raising climate inflation concerns. Oil only briefly dipped, supply-side shocks remain. Core inflation may not be as benign as recent PCE prints suggested.

factors.name: Real Yields Staying Elevated
factors.summary: With nominal rates sticky and inflation risks ticking higher, real yields remain elevated — headwind for long-duration growth/tech stocks. TIPS market not yet pricing dovish pivot; breakevens stable, suggesting no demand-driven disinflation narrative taking hold.

factors.name: FedEx Demand Signal Mixed
factors.summary: FedEx EVP stated 'I was not concerned...demand destruction has not at all been the case' — supporting economic resilience. However, this is a single data point; overall freight and logistics sentiment remains guarded given capex uncertainty.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation Weakening
factors.summary: Bitcoin holds above $60K but crypto equities (Coinbase, Circle) underperforming Big Tech; suggests risk-on appetite is selective and not broad-based. BTC strength not translating to broad NDX support, reducing traditional risk-on multiplier effect.

factors.name: China AI Competition Intensifying
factors.summary: Zhipu GLM 5.2 'closing in on top U.S. AI models' with Anthropic/OpenAI held back; suggests U.S. dominance premium in valuations may compress. No tariff relief signal yet from Trump administration on China semiconductors/AI exports.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Uptick
factors.summary: Tanker struck in Strait of Hormuz amid U.S.-Iran tensions despite '60-day ceasefire'; Hezbollah rejects Lebanon deal as 'surrender'; Venezuela earthquake killing ~1K. Supply chain/energy disruption risks rising, oil volatility will weigh on growth multiples.

factors.name: No Major Fed Dovish Signal
factors.summary: No FOMC in news window; latest PCE appeared to cool (referenced in 'Trading in these two ETFs suggests inflation fears are overblown') but core PCE not decisively lower. Fed unlikely to cut rates aggressively; rate cycle expectations remain stable to slightly higher.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 has sold off modestly (-0.75% 4h, -0.58% 24h) as the market reassesses the AI trade's sustainability. Jeremy Grantham's call that equities are at 'most expensive in American history' and widespread concerns about AI capex ROI (Oracle, memory costs, hyperscaler debt) are weighing on sentiment. Simultaneously, inflation risks are ticking back up — ECB warnings, European heat, oil supply disruptions in the Persian Gulf — which keeps real yields elevated and pressures long-duration growth stocks. The rally in tech that drove earlier 2026 gains is now being questioned; move is in early Phase 2 (pullback underway but not yet extended).
catalysts_ahead: Next major CPI/PCE release (likely July) — core inflation surprise would be deterministic
FOMC meeting (early July) — guidance on rate path and inflation outlook critical
Mega-cap earnings guidance (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA in Q3 season) — capex commentary
Semiconductor earnings (NVDA, TSMC, ASML) — AI demand and margin validation
Treasury yield repricing if inflation expectations rise further
Geopolitical escalation updates (Strait of Hormuz, U.S.-Iran, China)
Fed speakers and commentary on inflation, rates over next 2 weeks
screening.risk_warning: Bias flips BULLISH if: (1) Next CPI/PCE prints significantly cooler than expected, triggering Fed pivot narrative; (2) Mega-cap earnings beat with upside guidance on AI capex ROI and demand trajectory; (3) Real yields fall sharply due to flight-to-quality bid or dovish Fed signal; (4) Geopolitical risk subsides and Strait of Hormuz supply fears ease. Current move is early and could reverse quickly if macro data or earnings reassure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 with momentum down but not yet extended. Wait for retest of 29200–29300 resistance or a stabilization candle before shorting further; do not chase the dip unless clear break below 28900 with volume. Long entry only on confirmed break above 29300 with 4h close AND supportive macro news (cooler inflation, dovish Fed hint).

factors.name: Tech Equity Valuation Concerns
factors.summary: Jeremy Grantham called this 'the most expensive market in American history,' citing soaring AI-driven valuations. Bloomberg reported 'Tech Equity Sales Renew AI Debt-Binge Worries' with companies offloading stock amid concerns over rising semiconductor costs and memory pricing pressures.

factors.name: Semiconductor Cost Inflation
factors.summary: Reports highlight memory shortage creating 'existential crisis' for smaller players while AAPL and MSFT raise prices to cover soaring memory costs. This compresses margins and raises capex burden across hyperscalers, threatening guidance sustainability.

factors.name: Tech Earnings & Guidance Reset
factors.summary: Oracle posted worst week since dot-com bust (2001) as surging spending, negative free cash flow, and $130B debt pile weighed on stock. FedEx showed resilience on freight demand, but tech-heavy Nasdaq saw broader capitulation on capex sustainability concerns.

factors.name: AI Capex Sustainability Questions
factors.summary: Hyperscaler capex guidance is under scrutiny; concerns over AI returns on investment and rising power/memory costs are dampening enthusiasm. GE Vernova data center turbines and AI-linked energy demand are priced in, but ROI clarity is lacking.

factors.name: Macro Headwinds: Inflation & Fed Policy
factors.summary: ECB's Schnabel warned of 'upside inflation risks' despite peace deal reopening Strait of Hormuz. Heat waves in Europe and Middle East tensions (tanker strike, US-Iran ceasefire) create stagflation risks. Fed expectations remain fluid; real yields and long-duration tech sensitivity remain high-risk.

factors.name: Crypto & Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin trapped below $62.9K in bear trend; 50K BTC moved at loss; short-term holder stress at 2-year highs. Crypto weakness often precedes risk-off equity rotations. Stablecoin and DeFi uncertainties add contagion tail risk.

factors.name: Consumer Demand Softness Signals
factors.summary: Amazon Prime Day offers 'a glimpse into US consumer as shoppers navigate with pinched wallets.' FedEx avoided demand destruction but macro consumer health appears fragile; Red Lobster 'Endless Shrimp' was described as a 'car crash' for the company.

factors.name: Previous Sell Bias Confirmation
factors.summary: Your prior three 4h/18h reads (2026-06-27 14:29, 16:04, 18:02) all flagged SELL5/10 bias without confirmation trigger. Continued selling pressure (-0.75% 4h, -0.58% 24h) reinforces conviction, but lack of fresh catalyst is cautionary.

factors.name: Risk-On Reversal Possible
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO and 401(k) record levels suggest some retail optimism. Middle East ceasefire efforts (US-Iran, Israel-Lebanon talks) could reduce geopolitical premium. China stimulus and AI hype in certain names (Bittensor, GLP-1 regulation) provide pockets of support.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings Cycle Fatigue
factors.summary: Tech slump deepened this week on reassessment of AI trade sustainability. Micron's blockbuster earnings failed to support the group; Coinbase and Circle underperformed Big Tech, widening the valuation gap. Single-name reversal risk is real but sector momentum is down.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is grinding lower on a confluence of tech valuation concerns, semiconductor cost inflation, and AI capex sustainability doubts. Oracle's worst week since 2001 and Jeremy Grantham's 'most expensive market' call signal peak euphoria reversal. Cryptoweakness (Bitcoin sub-$63K, high short-term holder stress) and consumer pinch signals (Amazon Prime Day softness) add macro drag. Your sustained SELL5/10 bias over 4h intervals is holding, but lack of fresh catalyst or gap-down confirmation suggests a potential PHASE_2 chop; move is slow and grinding rather than capitulative.
catalysts_ahead: US PCE data (if due this week—check calendar for exact date)
FOMC meeting/guidance window (if approaching)
Mega-cap earnings: NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL next earnings dates TBD
Hyperscaler cloud capex guidance (AWS, Azure, GCP) on earnings calls
ECB policy signals or inflation data (EU CPI next)
Geopolitical escalation or de-escalation in Middle East (Strait of Hormuz traffic, Iran-US talks)

China economic data or stimulus announcements
screening.risk_warning: If mega-cap earnings beat significantly and forward capex guidance surprises high (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL), or if Fed signals imminent rate cuts, NQ100 could reverse to NEUTRAL/BUY quickly. Middle East peace breakthrough or real yield compression (10Y Treasury below 3.8%) would shift bias bullish. Move not yet extended enough to rule out false breakdown; watch for 29,000 support hold and any bounce-back to 29,400 resistance as confirmation of SELL thesis.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early-to-mid phase grinding down; avoid chasing shorts into gap-down. Wait for retest of 29,200–29,400 resistance for better short entry, or a break below 28,900 support for confirmation of continued downtrend. If rebound stalls at 29,300, re-engage on weakness.

factors.name: Tech equity selling pressure
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'Tech Equity Sales Renew AI Debt-Binge Worries' with tech companies selling stock aggressively; Oracle had worst week since 2001 dot-com bust on capex concerns and $130B debt pile. Growing skepticism about AI capex ROI.

factors.name: AI valuation reassessment
factors.summary: Jeremy Grantham calls this 'the most expensive market in American history' citing soaring AI valuations; multiple sources flag unsustainable capital spending by hyperscalers without proven returns, weighing on mega-cap tech sentiment.

factors.name: Semiconductor cost inflation
factors.summary: News highlights rising semiconductor costs, memory chip pricing pressures, and capex strain on AI infrastructure players; smaller consumer electronics firms face 'existential crisis' as Apple and Microsoft absorb costs via price increases.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk escalation
factors.summary: Fresh US-Iran military strikes, tanker attacks in Strait of Hormuz, and ceasefire breakdown create energy/supply-chain uncertainty; ECB's Schnabel warns of upside inflation risk despite peace talks. Risk-off sentiment weighs on growth equities.

factors.name: Crypto weakness and macro correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin stuck below $62,900; old ETH wallets moving holdings at losses; CoinDesk reports crypto selloff dragging gold down. BTC-NQ risk-off correlation intact; weakness in risk assets broadens NDX pressure.

factors.name: Real yields and duration sensitivity
factors.summary: No explicit 10Y Treasury or TIPS data in news window; inflation headlines (heat waves, Strait disruptions) suggest upside rate risk, but actual yield move not yet visible. Real rate dynamics remain uncertain.

factors.name: SpaceX Nasdaq inclusion (positive catalyst)
factors.summary: SpaceX fast-tracked to join NDX with massive ETF inflows expected; short-term positive flow driver for index. However, diluted by broader tech sentiment deterioration.

factors.name: 4h and 24h price action
factors.summary: Down 0.75% in 4h and 0.58% in 24h; modest move downward already in progress. Not extended, but directional bias intact.

factors.name: Bias continuity from prior reads
factors.summary: Three consecutive SELL5/10 reads at 16:04, 18:02, 20:08 on 2026-06-27 with no confirmation. Today's news (capex concerns, valuation excess, geopolitical risk) reinforces downdraft thesis without fresh intraday spike.

screening.narrative: NAS100 remains under pressure as the AI trade reassessment deepens. Jeremy Grantham's 'most expensive market in American history' call, coupled with Oracle's capex-debt crisis and widespread concerns over semiconductor cost inflation, is eroding conviction in mega-cap growth. Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran strikes, Strait of Hormuz disruptions) adds macro uncertainty and threatens energy/inflation outlook, further pressuring long-duration equities. SpaceX Nasdaq entry is a modest tailwind, but insufficient to offset the weight of valuation fears and capex anxiety dominating the narrative. Prior three SELL reads show consistency, though no confirmation spike has materialized yet—price action is grinding lower rather than capitulating.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting / Fed speakers (timing TBD in this week or next)
CPI/Core CPI print (likely early July)
Mega-cap earnings: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA guidance on capex / cloud demand
US-Iran geopolitical developments (sanctions, supply disruption confirmation)
TSMC / semiconductor supply chain updates on pricing / lead times
Macro economic data (NFP, retail sales, jobless claims) for growth narrative
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed signals rate cuts / dovish pivot → bonds rally, growth multiples re-rate higher; (2) AI capex fears prove overblown and hyperscaler earnings beat with robust guidance; (3) US-Iran ceasefire holds and Strait reopens → energy falls, inflation fears ease, real yields compress; (4) VIX spikes >25 and reverses to bid-on-dips (tactical risk-on flush); (5) mega-cap single-name catalyst (NVDA earnings beat, MSFT Azure strength) reignites momentum in select NDX components, fragmenting the sell-off.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (down 0.75% in 4h). Wait for retest of 29,250–29,400 resistance or fresh breakdown below 29,000 support before sizing; current -0.75% is not yet extended enough to justify aggressive chase. Confirm with 4h close below 29,050 before scaling short; consider profit-taking above 29,400 if no new negative catalyst emerges.
